items.headline: PLD Athena Phase B site work permitted at Rue Ferrari
items.summary: A $35 million commercial project receives permit for site work on the Athena development. The project scale suggests a substantial office or mixed-use complex in San José's commercial corridor.

items.headline: Bank of America tenant improvement permitted on Winchester
items.summary: Bank of America receives $2.4 million permit for office buildout at 560 S Winchester Bl, indicating a significant corporate presence or expansion in that corridor.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument artwork agreement approved
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust regarding the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The agreement establishes terms for the artwork's presence and maintenance at the public park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget set
items.summary: City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's FY 2026-27 budget report and annual assessment. The district funds improvements and services in that commercial corridor.

items.headline: City Council updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council is scheduled to review updates to the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan covering 2015–2040. The agenda does not provide details on what changes are being proposed or adopted.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager on May 19, 2026. This fills the position through a formal appointment process.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates water supply and drought contingency plans
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed drafts of the Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These plans guide the city's water supply strategy and outline actions to take if drought conditions worsen.

items.headline: City opens Spring 2026 grants for community events and neighborhood projects
items.summary: The City Council considered applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program's spring cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can apply for funding to host local events and neighborhood improvements.
